news.title: Vijayawada civic body posts ₹267 crore tax collection in FY26
news.para: Vijayawada Municipal Corporation achieved a significant tax collection of Rs 267.10 crore for the 2025-26 financial year. This marks a Rs 15 crore improvement over the previous year's collection.  The civic body's efforts in tracing vacant land tax owners and taking lawful actions contributed to this revenue growth. The target for the upcoming financial year is set at 90% collection.

news.title: Belagavi: Property tax collection of BCC rises to ₹70.8 crore, but target remains elusive
news.para: Belagavi City Corporation collected Rs 70.8 crore in property tax for FY 2025-26, falling short of its Rs 89.4 crore target. Officials cited election duties and census work as reasons for the shortfall. The target for FY 2026-27 is projected to exceed Rs 92 crore, with ongoing census work posing further challenges.

news.title: Lucknow development body survey's to curb fraud, update records
news.para: The Lucknow Development Authority (LDA) has initiated a door-to-door property verification drive to update its records and prevent fraud. A private agency is conducting the survey, collecting details like mobile numbers and ownership documents from residents. This initiative aims to digitize records, improve services, and mitigate risks of disputes and fraudulent transactions.

news.title: Amaravati is yet to have a civic body
news.para: Amaravati, the capital region, currently lacks a unified civic body. While legal status is settled, a single urban local body to govern its diverse areas remains a work in progress. Proposals for a Greater Vijayawada Municipal Corporation are pending. This consolidation aims for uniform and sustainable development across the capital's urban centers and villages.

news.title: Nashik civic body's property tax kitty swells 10% to ₹287 crore
news.para: Nashik Municipal Corporation achieved a record Rs287 crore in property tax collection for the financial year 2025-26. This marks a significant 10% increase from the previous year. The surge is attributed to successful amnesty schemes and rebates for timely taxpayers. The Cidco and Panchavati divisions led the collections.

news.title: Rajkot civic body misses property tax collection target
news.para: Rajkot Municipal Corporation's tax recovery branch fell short of its property tax target for FY 2025-26, collecting Rs 447.93 crore. Despite this, collections saw an 8.44% growth from the previous year, with a strong shift towards digital payments and strict enforcement against defaulters.

news.title: Nagpur civic body's stamp duty collection again falls short of target
news.para: Nagpur's stamp duty collection for property transactions in the last financial year was over Rs1,700 crore. This missed the Rs1,900 crore target by about 9%. The total value of transactions exceeded Rs24,000 crore. The state government has kept ready reckoner rates unchanged for the current financial year to boost realty purchases and increase stamp duty collections.

news.title: Andheri East tops in property tax collection in FY26, Lower Parel next in Mumbai
news.para: Mumbai's K-East ward led property tax collections for 2025-26, contributing to the BMC's record Rs 7,610.9 crore revenue. This achievement, surpassing targets, was driven by aggressive recovery drives and improved payment systems. Western suburbs dominated collections, highlighting property tax's crucial role as the BMC's primary income source.

news.title: Pune civic body misses revenue target for FY26 by ₹3,400 crore
news.para: Pune Municipal Corporation fell short of its Rs 12,600 crore revenue target for FY 2025-26, collecting only Rs 9,200 crore. A significant deficit stemmed from building permissions and property tax recovery issues, particularly in newly merged areas. Activists criticized reliance on amnesty schemes over strict action against defaulters.

news.title: Noida records highest stamp duty collections in a decade, but property registration slows
news.para: Noida's stamp and registration department achieved its highest revenue in a decade, collecting Rs 4,498.7 crore in FY 2025-26, despite a dip in property registrations. This success was driven by state government rebates on stamp duty for women and reduced fees for rental and family property transfers.

news.title: CM Rural Housing Scheme covers over 19,000 in Gorakhpur division
news.para: The Chief Minister Rural Housing Scheme is bringing permanent homes to thousands of underprivileged families in Gorakhpur division. Over 19,000 beneficiaries are covered, with more than 17,000 already in their new houses. The government has spent Rs 219 crore on this initiative. The scheme prioritizes families affected by natural disasters and vulnerable communities.

news.title: Over 4,200 flats in pipeline as Bengaluru development body prioritises vertical housing
news.para: The Bangalore Development Authority (BDA) is prioritizing apartment construction with six new housing projects totaling 4,251 flats across the city. These projects, valued at Rs 3,200 crore, aim to accommodate Bengaluru's rapid population growth through vertical development. The BDA has also completed over 13,000 flats and is enhancing transparency in its allotment process.

news.title: Gurugram civic body collects over ₹350 crore in property tax in FY26
news.para: Gurugram Municipal Corporation (MCG) collected over Rs 350 crore in property tax and Rs 56 crore in water bills for the 2025-26 financial year. Offices remained open on a holiday to facilitate last-minute payments, with arrangements made for senior citizens.

news.title: DLF Corporate Greens not ongoing project: Haryana RERA declines buyers' complaint
news.para: Haryana Rera has clarified its jurisdiction, ruling that projects completed before its establishment are not subject to registration. In a separate case, the authority upheld the finality of adjudicated disputes, dismissing a fresh compensation plea against Emaar India Ltd after a prior settlement for possession delays.

news.title: Parliament passes amendments to IBC to quicken resolution process, reduce backlog
news.para: Parliament has approved changes to the Insolvency and Bankruptcy Code. These amendments aim to speed up company resolutions and reduce pending cases. The Finance Minister highlighted the IBC's success in improving the banking sector and recovering assets. Scheduled Commercial Banks have seen significant recoveries through the IBC channel. The changes were approved by both the Lok Sabha and Rajya Sabha.

news.title: Delhi civic body's property tax collection crosses ₹3,100 crore in FY26
news.para: The Municipal Corporation of Delhi saw a significant 46 per cent jump in property tax collection for the 2025-26 financial year. This surge was driven by better taxpayer compliance, stricter enforcement, and a successful property amnesty scheme. The civic body also expanded its tax base by identifying new taxpayers and previously unassessed properties.

news.title: J&K government identified 578 illegal constructions in three years, granted 807 building permissions: CM
news.para: Jammu and Kashmir authorities issued 807 building permits across tourist destinations in the past three years. During the same period, 578 illegal constructions were identified and action was taken. The government also registered 2,613 homestays. GIS-based inventories are being maintained for authorized constructions in key tourist spots. Audits of illegal constructions have been conducted in some areas.

news.title: Prestige Group to jointly develop land parcel in Gurugram
news.para: Prestige Estates Projects has teamed up with Sare Gurugram to secure a major land deal in Gurugram. The agreement, which encompasses a sprawling 17.2-acre site in Sector 92, is set to yield roughly three million square feet of valuable development space.

news.title: Property rates in 2,624 locations in Indore to go up
news.para: Indore's property market is set for a price hike. New collector guideline rates will make real estate more expensive in over 2,000 areas starting Wednesday. This change follows a significant district-wide increase in property valuations. Residents rushed to register properties before the new rates. The real estate sector shows signs of a slowdown despite increased revenue.
